Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, indicating significant renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, elevated microalbumin/creatinine ratio, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance, supporting renal dysfunction. Hemoglobin A1c is low, suggesting possible recent hypoglycemia or anemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and further assessment of glycemic status.
